task_completion.description: Create the CDB list file containing the authorized user 'ga'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the creation or content of '/var/ossec/etc/lists/authorized_users' (e.g., via cat, nano, or echo) containing the user 'ga' (often in key:value format like 'ga:').

task_completion.description: Register the CDB list in the Wazuh manager configuration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing 'ossec.conf' being edited or viewed, with an XML tag like '<list>/var/ossec/etc/lists/authorized_users</list>' added to the <ruleset> section.

task_completion.description: Configure the custom rule with the correct ID, level, and parent SID in local_rules.xml.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing 'local_rules.xml' being edited or viewed, containing a rule definition with '<rule id="100500" level="12">' and a child tag '<if_sid>5715</if_sid>'.

task_completion.description: Configure the rule to check the authorized_users list and set the correct description.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the rule 100500 definition in 'local_rules.xml', look for a list check (e.g., '<list field="dstuser" lookup="not_match_key">etc/lists/authorized_users</list>') and the exact description tag '<description>Unauthorized SSH user login detected</description>'.

task_completion.description: Apply all changes by restarting the Wazuh manager.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a command executed to restart the service, such as 'systemctl restart wazuh-manager' or '/var/ossec/bin/wazuh-control restart'.

task_completion.description: Verify the logic using the provided sample logs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the execution of the Wazuh logtest tool (e.g., '/var/ossec/bin/wazuh-logtest') processing the file '/home/ga/ssh_test_logs.txt', with the output clearly showing rule 100500 triggering an alert for the unauthorized user.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely modified the Wazuh configuration files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the use of standard text editors (nano, vim) or shell redirection (>, >>) to modify ossec.conf and local_rules.xml, rather than just printing the expected XML to the console.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual Wazuh logtest utility to verify the logs.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should show the authentic output format of 'wazuh-logtest' (showing phases like 'Phase 1: Completed pre-decoding', 'Phase 2: Completed decoding', 'Phase 3: Completed filtering (rules)'), not a mocked or manually typed output.

integrity.description: The agent did not create fake scripts to simulate the restart or logtest process.
integrity.visual_evidence: Commands executed should be standard system commands and Wazuh binaries located in their proper directories (e.g., /var/ossec/bin/), without suspicious wrapper scripts being created or executed.
